name: Cocos (Keeling) Islands
summary: Cocos (Keeling) Islands is an Indian Ocean common-law Australian external territory — structurally distinctive globally as the only state-level Australian entity with majority Sunni Muslim Cocos Malay population (~75% of total population), retaining a distinct Cocos Malay (Bahasa Cocos) creole language. Population is composed primarily of descendants of Malay workers brought by the Clunies-Ross family from 1827 onwards. Family-law framework operates under Australian Family Law Act 1975 with Western Australian administrative arrangements applicable since the 1992 self-determination integration into Australia, supplemented by accommodations for Cocos Malay Islamic-law personal-status practices through community arrangements. Parental responsibility and child custody are governed by Australian family-law principles. The Federal Circuit and Family Court of Australia has jurisdiction; final appellate jurisdiction lies with the High Court of Australia. Family-law matters are heard at first instance in Cocos Islands Court arrangements with mainland Australian judicial support. Psychology profession is regulated through the Australian Health Practitioner Regulation Agency (AHPRA). Cocos (Keeling) Islands is silent on 'parental alienation' as a statutory label; courts operate substantively under the Australian best-interests-of-the-child standard. Cocos (Keeling) Islands is a Hague Convention 1980 party via Australian territorial extension effective 1 January 1987.

statutory_framework.citation: Australian Family Law Act 1975 (applicable via WA arrangements)
statutory_framework.title: Australian Family Law Act (applied via WA arrangements)
statutory_framework.relevance: Australian Family Law Act applicable to Cocos (Keeling) Islands via Western Australian administrative arrangements since 1992 self-determination integration.

statutory_framework.title: Cocos (Keeling) Islands Act
statutory_framework.relevance: Federal Cocos (Keeling) Islands Act establishing Australian territory status.

apex_courts.name: Federal Circuit and Family Court of Australia
apex_courts.role: Apex court for family-law matters from Cocos (Keeling) Islands.

apex_courts.name: High Court of Australia
apex_courts.role: Final appellate court for civil and criminal matters from Cocos (Keeling) Islands.

professional_regulators.name: Australian Health Practitioner Regulation Agency (AHPRA)
professional_regulators.role: Federal regulator of health and allied health professionals including clinical psychology.

anonymisation_convention: Cocos (Keeling) Islands family-court decisions are anonymised per Federal Circuit and Family Court practice using initials.

key_developments.title: Clunies-Ross settlement + Cocos-Malay-population-heritage framework
key_developments.description: Clunies-Ross family settled Cocos Islands from 1827 bringing Malay workers — substantively distinctive globally Cocos-Malay-population-heritage framework. Foundational substantive 200-year Cocos Malay-Sunni-Muslim population framework persisting through 21st century affecting subsequent constitutional-democratic-trajectory.

key_developments.title: British annexation + Crown-colony-status framework
key_developments.description: British annexation of Cocos Islands 31 March 1857 + Crown colony status — substantively distinctive Indian Ocean British-Crown-colony framework. Subsequent 1903 incorporation into Straits Settlements + 1942 Japanese WWII occupation. Foundational pre-1955-Australian-Act framework affecting subsequent constitutional-administrative-trajectory.

key_developments.title: Clunies-Ross hereditary-government grant + Queen-Victoria-perpetuity-grant framework
key_developments.description: Queen Victoria grant of Cocos Islands to Clunies-Ross family in perpetuity 1886 — substantively distinctive globally Crown-grant-of-territory-to-private-family-in-perpetuity framework. Substantive Clunies-Ross-dynasty governance through 1978 Australian-compulsory-acquisition. Foundational substantive 92-year Clunies-Ross-dynasty framework affecting subsequent constitutional-democratic-trajectory.

key_developments.title: Cocos (Keeling) Islands Act + Australian-territory-status framework
key_developments.description: Federal Cocos (Keeling) Islands Act 1955 (Cth) establishing Australian territory status effective 23 November 1955 — substantively distinctive Indian Ocean Australian-Cocos-Keeling-administration framework transferred from Singapore. Foundational pre-1978-Clunies-Ross-acquisition framework affecting subsequent constitutional-administrative-trajectory.

key_developments.title: Australian Family Law Act 1975 + best-interests-of-the-child framework
key_developments.description: Australian Family Law Act 1975 (Cth) applicable to Cocos (Keeling) Islands establishing federal family-law framework. Foundational substantive Australian-family-law-framework applicable via subsequent 1992 WA arrangements affecting family-law-implementation.

key_developments.title: Australian acquisition of Clunies-Ross-territory + end-of-Clunies-Ross-dynasty framework
key_developments.description: Australian compulsory acquisition of Clunies-Ross territory 1978 ending 92-year Clunies-Ross dynasty — substantively distinctive Indian Ocean compulsory-acquisition-of-private-family-territory framework. Substantive demographic and family-law-implementation framework affecting subsequent 1984 UN-supervised Act of Self-Determination.

key_developments.title: UN-supervised Act of Self-Determination + vote-to-integrate-with-Australia framework
key_developments.description: UN-supervised Act of Self-Determination 6 April 1984 — Cocos Malay population voted to integrate with Australia (rather than independence or association). Substantively distinctive globally only-state-level-entity-to-vote-for-full-integration-with-metropolitan-state-in-UN-supervised-self-determination-process framework. Substantive constitutional-democratic-trajectory framework affecting subsequent reform-trajectory.

key_developments.title: Hague Convention 1980 territorial extension + UNCRC-Australia-extension framework
key_developments.description: Hague Convention 1980 territorial extension by Australia to Cocos (Keeling) Islands effective 1 January 1987 — establishing Indian Ocean Hague Convention jurisdiction framework. Australia ratified UNCRC 17 December 1990 with extension to Cocos (Keeling) Islands — establishing best-interests-of-the-child substantive doctrine within Australian-derivative common-law framework.

key_developments.title: WA administrative arrangements + Cocos-Keeling-Islands-WA-applied-law framework
key_developments.description: Western Australian administrative arrangements applied to Cocos (Keeling) Islands 1992 via Cocos (Keeling) Islands Act amendments — substantively reshaping family-law-implementation framework with WA-applied-law mechanism. Substantive WA-Family-Law-applicable framework affecting subsequent constitutional-democratic-trajectory.

key_developments.title: Albanese government + Shire-of-Cocos-Keeling-Islands + Commonwealth Ombudsman + continuing-Cocos-Malay-customary-Islamic framework
key_developments.description: Anthony Albanese (ALP) Federal Government framework continuing through 2023 with Shire of Cocos (Keeling) Islands institutional positions addressing PA-related issues within Australian Family Law Act 1975 + WA arrangements 1992 framework supplemented by Cocos Malay Islamic-law personal-status community accommodations. Commonwealth Ombudsman institutional positions affecting cross-border family-law adjudication. Continuing Bahasa Cocos creole language framework substantively significant institutional context. Substantively significant Indian Ocean Australian-external-territory institutional jurisprudential development.

key_developments.title: Federal Circuit and Family Court of Australia + High Court — best-interests-of-the-child substantive register + Cocos-Malay-Sunni-Muslim-majority context
key_developments.description: Federal Circuit and Family Court of Australia (Perth covering Cocos (Keeling) Islands) and High Court of Australia continue to develop best-interests-of-the-child jurisprudence under Family Law Act 1975 + WA arrangements 1992 + Hague Convention 1980 framework in custody disputes within Cocos-Malay-Sunni-Muslim-majority (~75%) context + Bahasa Cocos creole language framework. Substantive analysis without doctrinal 'parental alienation' label adoption.

structural_findings: Cocos (Keeling) Islands operates a common-law framework with Australian external territory status — places Cocos in the Australian external territory cluster.
Majority Sunni Muslim Cocos Malay population (~75%) is structurally distinctive globally — only state-level Australian entity with Muslim majority within the corpus.
Cocos Malay (Bahasa Cocos) creole language as distinct linguistic heritage is structurally distinctive.

UN-supervised self-determination Act 1984 with vote to integrate with Australia is structurally distinctive — only state-level entity to vote for full integration with metropolitan state in a UN-supervised self-determination process in the corpus.
Hague Convention 1980 applicability via Australian territorial extension reflects external-territory Hague jurisdiction status.

sources.title: Federal Circuit and Family Court of Australia

editorial_notes: Cocos (Keeling) Islands jurisdiction sidecar v1.1 — deepened 2026-06-09 from 5 to 10 key_developments with full Clunies-Ross-settlement-1827-to-continuing-Cocos-Malay-Sunni-Muslim-majority trajectory: 1827-Clunies-Ross-settlement + 1857-British-annexation + 1886-Clunies-Ross-hereditary-government-grant + 1955-Cocos-Keeling-Islands-Act + 1975-Australian-Family-Law-Act + 1978-Australian-acquisition-of-Clunies-Ross-territory + 1984-UN-supervised-Act-of-Self-Determination + 1987-Hague-Convention-+-UNCRC + 1992-WA-administrative-arrangements + 2024-Federal-Circuit-and-Family-Court-+-High-Court-best-interests.

Common-law Indian Ocean Australian external territory (Australian Family Law Act 1975 + Cocos (Keeling) Islands Act 1955 + UN 1984 self-determination + Cocos Malay majority population ~75% + Bahasa Cocos + Hague via Australian territorial extension 1987 + WA-applied-law framework 1992). Only majority-Muslim Australian entity globally + only state-level UN-supervised vote for full integration globally + only Crown grant of territory to private family in perpetuity (Clunies-Ross 1886-1978) globally distinctive.

PA-recognition: silent statutory + no-apex-position + silent regulator — substantive best-interests-of-the-child analysis under Family Law Act 1975 + WA arrangements 1992 + Hague Convention 1980 framework without doctrinal 'parental alienation' label.

Joins Indian-Ocean + common-law + Australian-external-territory-cluster + only-majority-Muslim-Australian-entity-globally-distinctive + Cocos-Malay-Sunni-Muslim-population-75-percent + Bahasa-Cocos-creole-language + Clunies-Ross-settlement-1827-+-Clunies-Ross-dynasty-1886-1978-Crown-grant-in-perpetuity-globally-distinctive + British-annexation-1857-+-Crown-colony-status + transferred-from-Singapore-to-Australia-1955 + Australian-acquisition-of-Clunies-Ross-territory-1978 + UN-supervised-Act-of-Self-Determination-1984-only-state-level-entity-to-vote-for-full-integration-globally-distinctive + Federal-Circuit-and-Family-Court-Perth-jurisdiction + Hague-via-Australian-territorial-extension clusters within the corpus.
